what_it_does: This file is a channel-memory attachment metadata record that links an attachment ID to a design-assets channel image target via a URI and records when it was added.
observations: YAML frontmatter contains id att-48cf0f32, channel design-assets, attachment_type images, target image://design-assets/icons/image (36), and an added timestamp 2026-06-22T12:26:25.829719+00:00.
The file body after the frontmatter is empty.
The target URI includes a parenthetical numeric index (36).
standouts: The target URI image://design-assets/icons/image (36) points to a specific indexed image asset.
The added timestamp is precise to microseconds with timezone.
concerns: The target title 'image (36)' is generic and gives no semantic description of the asset.
There is no context about why this attachment was added or which message it belongs to.
The meaning of the '(36)' index is not explained in the file.
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that records an image reference to design-assets icon index 36 with a precise creation timestamp.
questions: What does the '(36)' in the target URI represent—version, sequence, or gallery index?
Which channel message or conversation does this attachment belong to?
Is 'image (36)' the canonical name of the asset, or is it an auto-generated placeholder?
purpose_vs_actual: The file does not state an explicit purpose beyond its fields; its actual content is a minimal, typed attachment record, which aligns with the frontmatter schema.
intention_signals: It appears intended to create a durable, timestamped reference from a channel attachment to a specific design-assets image asset.
novelty: The target URI uses a numeric parenthetical index (36) within an image:// scheme, which is the only distinctive detail in an otherwise standard attachment metadata record.
contribution: It contributes a timestamped, typed attachment record that links a channel memory attachment to a specific design-assets image target via a URI.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory attachment metadata file for the design-assets channel that references image asset image://design-assets/icons/image (36) with an added timestamp.
